title: School Holiday: J&K extends school closure till July 26 as IMD forecasts heavy rain; Himachal,... - Moneycontrol.com
full_text:

The Jammu and Kashmir government has extended summer holidays for schools until July 26, 2026, as the southwest monsoon gathers strength across northern India and the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts widespread rainfall in the Himalayan region. The decision affects all government and recognised private schools in the Kashmir Division and the Winter Zones of the Jammu Division, with classes now scheduled to resume on Monday, July 27.
The extension comes after schools were initially expected to reopen on July 20 following summer vacations that began on July 6 due to an intense heatwave. While Jammu and Kashmir has revised its academic calendar in view of changing weather conditions, no statewide school closure orders have been announced in neighbouring Himachal Pradesh or Uttarakhand so far.
The announcement was made on Tuesday by Jammu and Kashmir Education Minister Sakina Ittoo, who said the decision had been taken with the safety of students, teachers and their families as the highest priority.
In a post on X, the minister said that summer vacations for all government and recognised private schools in the Kashmir Division and Winter Zones of the Jammu Division would continue until July 26, 2026.
She also urged residents to remain cautious and follow all advisories issued by the concerned authorities as the weather situation develops.
Schools across the affected regions will reopen on July 27, subject to any further directions issued by the administration.
According to the India Meteorological Department's forecast issued on July 22, fairly widespread to widespread rainfall is expected across Jammu, Kashmir, Ladakh, Gilgit, Baltistan and Muzaffarabad between July 22 and July 24.
The weather department has also forecast widespread rainfall over Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand from July 22 to July 27 as monsoon activity intensifies across northern India.
The forecast has prompted authorities to remain on alert and closely monitor the weather situation in the Himalayan states.
Although heavy rainfall has been forecast, the governments and education departments of Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand have not issued any statewide orders announcing school closures or holiday extensions.
Students, parents and school authorities have been advised to monitor official notifications issued by their respective state governments, district administrations and education departments, as any decision regarding the suspension of classes will depend on local weather conditions.
School closure decisions are generally taken based on district-level assessments rather than statewide advisories.
Authorities have also cautioned the public against relying on unverified messages circulating on social media regarding school closures. Students and parents have been advised to refer only to official announcements for the latest updates as the monsoon progresses across the region.
